subject: Geography
title: John Snow's Cholera Map
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on John Snow's Cholera Map.

hook.text: In 1854, cholera was killing hundreds in London. Doctors blamed 'bad air.'

buildup.text: John Snow plotted each death on a street map and saw them cluster around one water pump.

discovery.text: He convinced officials to remove the pump handle. The outbreak stopped.

twist.text: Snow had invented epidemiology—using maps to track disease—decades before germ theory.

climax.text: A simple dot map saved hundreds of lives and launched a new science.

punchline.text: One map, one pump, one breakthrough.

quiz.question: What did John Snow's map reveal about the cholera outbreak?
options: Deaths clustered around a single water pump
The disease spread through the air
